all_unique_row_labels:
3.2 Section A: Practice Problems - Problem 5
Family Connection: Peça que a família peça à criança para encontrar dois objetos em casa e decidir qual cobre mais espaço, usando as palavras 'mais espaço'/'more space' e desenhar as figuras para comparar.

Activity 1: Compare Shapes (15 minutes). Activity 2: Pattern Blocks to Compare Shapes (20 minutes). Students compare shapes and decide which is larger by covering with pattern blocks.
Co-Teaching Model: Station Teaching
Phase Plan:
  Warmup (10 min)
  Bilingual: Introduce bilingual vocabulary and model covering shapes using pattern blocks; demonstrate comparative language with frames.
  Primary: Lead quick practice identifying shapes and guide initial predictions about which covers more space.
  Stations - Compare & Measure (25 min)
  Bilingual: Support a small group measuring area with pattern blocks, prompting students to describe choices using 'more space'/'mais espaço' and count tiles.
  Primary: Observe and challenge groups to justify choices with evidence; circulate to other groups.
  Closure (10 min)
  Bilingual: Facilitate a 2-student compare share using frames: 'Shape A covers more space because _.'
  Primary: Summarize misconceptions (e.g., length vs area) and reinforce area language.
ELL Support:
  Concrete Representations with Pattern Blocks (Levels 1-3): Provide pattern blocks and guide students to cover shapes while counting tiles; bilingual teacher models counting in Portuguese then English.
  Comparative Sentence Frames (Levels 2-4): Frames: 'Shape A covers more space than Shape B because _.' Display frames in Portuguese and English and practice chorally.
  Visual Anchor Chart (Length vs Area) (Levels 2-4): Show side-by-side images demonstrating length vs area with bilingual labels; refer to chart during student reasoning.
Materials: Pattern blocks sets, Pairs of shapes printed for comparing, Visual anchor chart with bilingual labels (area/área; length/comprimento)

Assessment: 3.2.1 Cool Down
Instrument: Cool Down: orally explain which shape covers more space and why, using informal tools (pattern blocks).
Supports by Level:
  Levels 1-2: Allow students to show covered shapes and point to more tiles; permit response in Portuguese with teacher transcription to English.
  Levels 3-4: Provide comparative sentence frame and allow students to draw covered shapes and label counts.
  Levels 5-6: Expect students to orally explain using comparative connectors and correct use of 'area' vocabulary in English.

Content: Students will explain orally how to determine which shape covers the most space using informal tools and language.
Student Goal: I will explain which shape covers more space.
WIDA/Bilingual: Students will use language to explain reasoning about area and comparison (ELD-MA.2-3.Explain.Expressive) by using pattern blocks and comparative sentence frames appropriate for WIDA levels 2-4, producing an oral explanation and a labeled comparison.

LESSON 1: WHAT IS AREA?   
3.2.1 Teacher Guide

Students may believe length alone is enough to compare two-dimensional shapes rather than considering area.
Linguistic Note: Portuguese uses 'comprimento' (length) and 'área' (area) distinctly; students may conflate terms when reasoning in English.
Prevention: Explicitly contrast 'length' and 'area' with bilingual examples and have students demonstrate coverings with tiles while saying both terms.

Unit, Lesson #, Module:
05 - Fifth Grade Unit Description and Summary of Key Learning

Warm-up: How Many Do You See: Arrays (10 minutes)
Bilingual Bridge: Preview cognates: 'area - área', 'shape - forma', 'cover - cobrir'. Show shapes covered with pattern blocks and name them in Portuguese then English.
